One Link
How it works Features Security Share Get One Link
Features

Everything One Link can do, told by One Link.

This page is generated from the live capability advertisement of the One Link daemon at /api/capabilities. We cannot lie on this page. If a feature is here, the daemon ships it. If it is not, it does not.

live from /api/capabilities updated 2s ago
For people

The everyday things.

Messages

Only you and they can read it. Forward secrecy. Edit, delete, react. Read receipts that you control.

Files of any size

Drag a folder, a movie, a hard drive's worth. Peer-to-peer with per-chunk AEAD and fountain-coded loss recovery. Resumes on reconnect. Never copies to a server. 10 GB verified in the torture simulator.

Voice and video calls

End-to-end encrypted. Direct peer-to-peer when possible, relayed only when not. No mystery server in the path.

Shared folders

Drop a file in, it appears on the other side. Versioned, conflict-free CRDT, stored on your devices. Works on LAN and across air-gapped networks (mDNS + Bluetooth aware).

Your devices act as one

Phone, laptop, tablet, second laptop. Pair once with QR + five words. Every device sees the same conversations, end-to-end between every pair.

The promises, kept by the architecture

What the math guarantees.

Each line is a property the architecture enforces, not a policy someone could change later. The crate or proof behind each one is named and audit-ready.

No account, no email, no phone number.

Identity is a keypair on your device. There is no user database anywhere we could be subpoenaed for. Crate: ol_pair_qr. QR + 5-word SAS pairing.

End-to-end encrypted by default.

Cleartext never leaves your device. Every message, every chunk. Per-message forward secrecy active today; per-chunk activation with the chunk-store cutover in v0.22. Crates: ol_ratchet, ol_transfer.

No central server in the path.

Direct peer-to-peer when possible, encrypted-relay forwarding when not. Relays we run are just nodes; the network keeps running if every one of ours disappears. Anyone can run a relay.

Post-quantum hybrid keys + signatures.

Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 hybrid signatures live everywhere today. X25519 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid keys live on Sphinx onion hops today; the pair handshake and channel root activate the ML-KEM half in v0.22 (the wire format already reserves the slot). If either half breaks, the other still holds. Crates: ol_pqkem, ol_pqsig, ol_onion.

Reproducible signed builds + attestation.

Every release built in a sealed environment with an Ed25519-signed attestation document. Anyone can rebuild from source and verify byte equality. Verify in your tab on the download page. Crate: ol_confidential. Software baseline today, Windows TPM in flight, SE / SGX / SEV-SNP / TrustZone via the same trait.

Threshold recovery.

Split your identity across k of n trusted shares. Survive a lost device without surviving a leak. Crate: ol_threshold_recovery. BN multi-sig with per-signer R values.

Free forever, no business model.

No tier. No subscription. No "pro". No upsell. The network is community-run, the project is community-run, the cost to use it is zero forever.

Source open and verifiable.

AGPL-3.0. Every crate, every shader, every word of this site. github.com/IamOneYouAreOneWeAreOne/one-link.

Coming next

What lands in v0.22.

The crates ship today as WASM-verifiable primitives. The daemon-level toggles wire in v0.22.

Three-hop private routing

One toggle. Sphinx-routed through volunteer relays. Post-quantum blinded at every hop. No one (including us) sees who sent what to whom.

Duress mode

A second passphrase opens a believable empty account. The math gives you a way out under coercion. Primitive crate shipped; daemon wiring in v0.22.

GPU coherence field

The τ_c routing field already solves at 10k peers in 1.74ms on a single CPU core. The GPU path moves it to handheld real-time at million-peer scale.

Last reviewed 2026-05-19.

Get One Link → Read the threat model
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
